mission.title: kube-vip: Consistent DHCP renew failure analysis
mission.description: Consistent DHCP renew failure analysis. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current kube-vip deployment
steps.description:
Verify your kube-vip version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -A | grep kube-vip
kubectl get daemonset -n kube-system kube-vip-ds 2>/dev/null || echo "May be running as a static pod"
```
This feature requires a working kube-vip installation.

steps.title: Review kube-vip configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kube-vip configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -A | grep kube-vip
kubectl get configmap -n kube-system kube-vip 2>/dev/null
```
Potentially related issues: #572, #580

> ### **TL;DR** 
> **In certain network setups, DHCP renewals performed by `kube-vip` generally fail.** Further implementation issues result in ARP announcements of an IP for which a lease is no longer held.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Consistent DHCP renew failure analysis
steps.description:
When using DHCP for LB services, the resulting IP was assigned to the `serviceInterface` while most (not all) DHCP communication was done through a per-service `macvlan` interface that is created on demand.

This has been the cause of DHCP lease renewal failures for some users, as the response
```yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: kube-vip
    name: kube-vip
  name: kube-vip
  namespace: kube-vip
spec:
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: kube-vip
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: kube-vip
    spec:
      containers:
      - args:
        - manager
        env:
        - name: svc_enable
          value: "true"
        - name: vip_arp
          value: "true"
        - name: vip_leaderelection
          value: "true"
        - name: vip_serv
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -A | grep kube-vip
kubectl get events -n kube-system --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Consistent DHCP renew failure analysis" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
When using DHCP for LB services, the resulting IP was assigned to the `serviceInterface` while most (not all) DHCP communication was done through a per-service `macvlan` interface that is created on demand.

This has been the cause of DHCP lease renewal failures for some users, as the response packets from the server never reached kube-vip. For more details, see issue #871.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  labels:
    app.kubernetes.io/instance: kube-vip
    name: kube-vip
  name: kube-vip
  namespace: kube-vip
spec:
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/instance: kube-vip
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app.kubernetes.io/instance: kube-vip
    spec:
      containers:
      - args:
        - manager
        env:
        - name: svc_enable
          value: "true"
        - name: vip_arp
          value: "true"
        - name: vip_leaderelection
          value: "true"
        - name: vip_servicesinterface
          value: eth0
        - name: svc_election
          value: "true"
        - name: cp_namespace
          value: kube-vip
        - name: enable_node_labeling
          value: "t

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kube-vip installed as a static pod or DaemonSet.
